name: Sulbactam
executiveSummary: Beta-lactamase inhibitor used as monotherapy for *Acinetobacter baumannii* infections according to institutional protocols.
indications:
## Indications

- Monotherapy in *Acinetobacter baumannii* infections (intrinsic activity against OXA-23 and similar enzymes).
- May be combined with colistin or meropenem depending on sensitivity.

## Precautions

- Do not confuse with ampicillin-sulbactam.

> Adjust according to institutional protocols and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Vial with lyophilized powder containing 1 g of sodium sulbactam (Sulbactam Richet, Northia, others).
adulto.reconstitution: 10 mL of sterile water (SW) or normal saline (NS). Concentration: 100 mg/mL.
adulto.diluent: 1 g in 100 mL of NS or 5% dextrose (D5W).
adulto.administration: Direct IV: Yes. Reconstitute in 10 mL SW and administer slowly over 3–5 min. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute in 50–100 mL NS or D5W and infuse over 15–30 min.
adulto.notes: Usual dose: 3–4 g/day in 3–4 doses or prolonged infusion (1 g every 6 h over 3 h). May be combined with colistin or meropenem depending on sensitivity. Do not confuse with ampicillin-sulbactam.

pediatrico.dose: Regimens according to pediatric infectious disease protocols.

stability:
## Stability

- Reconstituted (in vial): 8 h at room temperature, 48 h refrigerated.
- Diluted solution (to be administered): 8 h at room temperature, 48 h refrigerated.

bibliography.citation: Infectious Diseases Service, Infection Prevention and Control. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Argentine Society of Infectious Diseases (SADI). Guidelines and consensus statements.
